methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
FlyBy Robotics is a Seed-stage defense and security robotics company founded by Jason Lu and Cat Orman, having raised $5M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp set of 10 private and 2 public peers in the Defense & Security sector, including same-stage and adjacent-stage companies such as Tenna Systems ($60M), Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Stark Defence ($500M), and Scale AI ($29.0B).

Two triangulation methods apply directly. The Stage-Progression method uses the $5M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding $12M. The Peer-Median approach examines five comparable companies across the same sector and stage band; the median valuation across this set is $300M, with a range spanning $60M to $29.0B—a wide dispersion reflecting heterogeneity in business scope and maturity within the defense tech category.

Synthesizing these two methods produces a triangulated range of $11M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $156M. The wide spread reflects meaningful uncertainty around FlyBy's positioning within the peer set. The Stage-Progression method anchors the lower bound, while the Peer-Median upper bound is influenced by outlier valuations at Series A. A defensible central estimate would cluster toward $50M–$150M, contingent on FlyBy's product differentiation, addressable market traction, and go-to-market clarity relative to the peer cohort.
